en: The crisis goes back a long way. It goes back really to the 1970s. By the 1970s, the two political parties, which are not really that different, they're both business parties with somewhat different emphasis.

So back in the 1960s, you couldn't tell a moderate Republican from a moderate Democrat. They're almost interchangeable. I voted for Republicans back in the 60s. It's inconceivable now. Starting in the 1960s, both parties underwent a change.
This was part of larger factors taking place in the global economy. The Republicans simply went off the rails. By now, they're not a political party in any ordinary sense.
